lesson.title: Traffic Law & Licensing
lesson.explanation: Learner's licence: age 17 (light motor vehicle), test at driving licence testing centre (DLTC), R78 fee, requires eye test + written test on road signs, rules, and vehicle controls. Driving licence: K53 practical test (yard test + road test), R120 fee. Licence valid 5 years (card), renewew at DLTC. AARTO points system: 12 demerit points = licence suspended (e.g., speeding 1-3 points, drunk driving 6 points). Blood alcohol limit: 0.05g/100ml (breath: 0.24mg/L). Roadworthy certificate required for vehicle registration transfer. Cell phone use while driving: R1,500 fine.

misconceptions.misconception: You don't need formal training for Traffic Law & Licensing
misconceptions.correction: Proper training and certification are required for safety and legal compliance in SA.
misconceptions.why_students_think_this: People see informal work and assume no qualifications are needed.
